summary_text: 1) [High] Multiple helper paths write Notion directly without local-queue write-back, so Notion becomes mutable but the queue (intended local source of truth for runtime state) can diverge. - `apply_post_package.py:133-170` writes PATCH via `_notion_request` (including content/title/body + thumbnail fields), and no local post record is updated after success. - `scripts/sync_post_image.py:58-72` and `scripts/upsert_notion_draft.py:35-40` also PATCH Notion directly. - `manual_publish.py:65-80` and `manual_publish.py:157-160` patch thumbnail metadata and image to Notion without queue reconciliation. - `reject_handler.py:55-59` mutates Notion status to `draft`, then only updates local JSON for that same post. - Compliance risk: same post can exist in conflicting states across Notion and `post_queue` after one writer succeeds and another fails/retries. 2) [High] `scheduler` drops the active queue when Notion returns an empty result set instead of preserving local rows. - `scheduler.py:466-469` sets `posts = []` when `load_posts_from_notion()` succeeds but is empty. - `post_generator.py:273-287` also returns empty list on successful empty Notion pull. - Compliance risk: transient Notion empties (or permission/filter misses) can erase local/unsynced work and stall posting unexpectedly. 3) [High] Field-policy is inconsistent between operational sync and helper sync paths. - Core sync path intentionally limits writes to operational deltas in `notion_sync._build_dirty_notion_properties` + `push_updates_to_notion` (`notion_sync.py:369-382`, `543-550`). - Helper paths call `_post_record_to_notion_properties` (`notion_sync.py:318-366`), which includes full content/title/body/thumbnails and can push non-operational fields. - Compliance risk: helper and scheduler flows are not governed by same SSOT policy, increasing silent overwrite and rollback complexity. 4) [Medium] Image sync updates Notion but not the local queue metadata lifecycle consistently. - `image_sync.py:14-107` uploads and updates Notion `Image`, but returns only a URL; no queue-field persistence (`thumbnail_image_present`, `_image_path`, `thumbnail_*`, etc.). - `apply_post_package._sync_image` and `scripts/sync_post_image` set Notion thumbnail fields (`...:151-171`, `:62-71`) but never persist those back into local queue records. - Compliance risk: scheduler may repeatedly treat thumbnails as unresolved or stale and trigger rework/retries. 5) [Medium] Local queue is only partially supplemented when Notion is SoT. - `scheduler.py:451-465` and `load_posts_from_json.py:283-287` only backfill `image_path`/`reply_url` (plus dirty dirty-state fields), but do not reconcile other local-only queue state (e.g., thumbnail fields) after fresh Notion pulls. - Compliance risk: local operational metadata can be lost or treated as unknown after Notion refresh cycles. 6) [Medium] Single-writer enforcement is not actually used by all writers. - `notion_sync.py` advertises “single writer” behavior (`load/save` and sync helpers) but helper scripts bypass it with direct `_notion_request` calls (`scripts above`), bypassing centralized policy/validation. - Compliance risk: the “Notion DB as controller” claim is not end-to-end true. **Recommended changes (priority):** - Introduce a single Notion write function in `notion_sync.py` that performs `PATCH -> optional GET verify -> queue update (including `_notion_last_edited_time` and dirty flag clear)` and replace direct `_notion_request` patch calls in: - `scripts/apply_post_package.py:133-171` - `scripts/sync_post_image.py:58-72` - `scripts/upsert_notion_draft.py:35-40` - `manual_publish.py:65-80`, `manual_publish.py:152-160` - `reject_handler.py:55-64` - `scripts/local/integrations/xauto-sync-approved-thumbnail.py:25-44`, `:56-71` - In `scheduler.py:466-469` and `post_generator.py:273-287`, never treat empty Notion result as authoritative “empty queue”; merge local non-posted rows (especially `notion_page_id` missing/invalid + unsynced local) into wo...
